pageTimings.text: Wren wanted to be taller. She had found a spell in her book. A growing spell, she was sure. She read it very carefully.
She said the spell. There was a flash of bright blue light. Then it was quiet.
Wren looked at her hands. Her hands were a very long way away. She was not taller. She was one inch tall.
Oh, said Wren. Her voice came out very small. Everything around her was enormous.
Wren found her spell book. It was enormous now. She climbed up onto it and looked at the spell. She had read it backwards.
Right, said Wren. She walked to the beginning of the spell. She read it again. This time, she read it forwards.
Luna flew in through the window. She looked at the desk. She looked more closely. "Wren?" said Luna.
"Fixing it," said tiny Wren. She raised her tiny hands. She said the spell again. Forwards, this time.
There was another flash of blue. Wren was back to her normal size. She brushed off her robe. She straightened her hat.
She looked at herself carefully. She was exactly the same height as before. The growing spell had not worked at all.
"Did it work?" asked Pip. "No," said Wren. "I am exactly the same height."
Wren picked up her spell book. She turned to the next spell. "But I am going to find a better one," she said. She began to read very, very carefully. Forwards.
